example of monotonic function example of monotonic function example of monotonic function example of monotonic function example of monotonic function A monotonic function is a mathematical function that preserves a given order between its domain and range. Specifically, a function \( f \) is said to be monotonic if it is either entirely non-increasing or non-decreasing. This means that for any two points in the domain of the function, if the first point is less than the second, then the value of the function at the first point will not exceed (for non-decreasing functions) or will not be less than (for non-increasing functions) the value at the second point.  An example of a monotonic function is \( f(x) = x^3 \).
